_meta.verificationNote: AI Generated: Controls library for BNM Management of Customer Information and Permitted Disclosures. All content is indicative and should be validated against the FSP's operating context, risk appetite, and proportionality requirements.

_source.sourceDocument: Management of Customer Information and Permitted Disclosures (BNM/RH/PD 028-65)

controls.name: Board Tone-at-the-Top on Customer Information Protection
controls.description: The board sets and communicates clear expectations on the importance of safeguarding customer information, establishing a culture of data protection accountability across the FSP.
keyActivities: Board formally communicates the strategic importance of customer information protection
Board sets expectations on consequences for customer information breaches
Customer information protection is embedded in the FSP's corporate values and code of conduct
Board exercises active oversight on all matters related to customer information handling

maturity.basic: Board acknowledges customer information protection as important but no formal communication or documented expectations exist
maturity.mature: Board issues annual statement on customer information protection, expectations documented in board charter, regular board discussions on data protection posture
maturity.advanced: Board-driven culture programme with measurable KPIs on customer information protection, regular board engagement with frontline staff, public commitment to data stewardship

controls.name: Board Approval of Customer Information Policies
controls.description: The board formally approves all written policies governing customer information protection and ensures that comprehensive policies, procedures, and controls are in place for confidentiality and security.
keyActivities: Board reviews and formally approves customer information protection policies
Board verifies that policies cover the complete customer information lifecycle
Board ensures procedures and controls are adequate for confidentiality and security
Approval records are maintained with board minutes and resolutions

maturity.basic: Policies exist but board approval is informal or undocumented
maturity.mature: Formal board approval process with structured review of policy adequacy, documented resolutions, and annual re-approval cycle
maturity.advanced: Board-level policy governance framework with policy effectiveness metrics, benchmarking against regulatory expectations, and proactive policy enhancement

controls.name: Board Oversight and Management Reporting
controls.description: Board reviews senior management reports on customer information management including risk assessments, findings, and mitigation plans, with periodic reviews to reflect changes in the operating environment.
keyActivities: Senior management provides structured reports to the board on customer information management
Reports include risk assessments, audit findings, and mitigation plans
Board satisfies itself that policies, procedures, and controls are adequate and effective
Periodic review cycle ensures reports reflect evolving risks and operating environment changes

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc reporting to board on customer information matters with no standard format or cadence
maturity.mature: Quarterly structured reporting with standard template covering risk assessments, incidents, and mitigation status, board documented challenge and follow-up
maturity.advanced: Real-time dashboard reporting, trend analysis, predictive risk indicators, and board-level scenario analysis on emerging customer information threats

controls.name: Annual Written Assurance from Senior Management
controls.description: The board obtains annual written assurance from senior management confirming that customer information controls are effective and that outsourced service providers meet their contractual safeguarding obligations.
keyActivities: Senior management prepares annual written assurance on control effectiveness
Assurance covers both internal controls and OSP contractual compliance
Board reviews and formally acknowledges the assurance statement
Gaps or caveats in assurance are tracked with remediation plans

maturity.basic: Informal verbal assurance with no written documentation
maturity.mature: Formal annual written assurance signed by CEO/CRO with supporting evidence, documented board review, and gap tracking
maturity.advanced: Continuous assurance model with automated control testing evidence, third-party validation, and quantified assurance metrics

controls.name: Customer Information Threat and Vulnerability Identification
controls.description: Systematic identification and remediation of threats and vulnerabilities that could result in theft, loss, misuse, or unauthorised access, modification, or disclosure of customer information.
keyActivities: Identify internal and external threats to customer information
Assess vulnerabilities across systems, processes, and people
Classify threats as negligent or deliberate in origin
Develop and implement remediation plans for identified vulnerabilities

maturity.basic: Reactive identification of threats only after incidents occur, no systematic vulnerability scanning
maturity.mature: Annual threat and vulnerability assessment with structured methodology, covering internal, external, negligent, and deliberate threat sources
maturity.advanced: Continuous threat intelligence integration, automated vulnerability discovery, red-team exercises specifically targeting customer information assets

controls.name: Likelihood and Impact Assessment
controls.description: Formal assessment of the likelihood of identified threats materialising and their potential impact on the FSP and customers, including third-party and outsourcing risks, supported by mitigation plans.
keyActivities: Assess likelihood of each identified threat materialising
Evaluate potential impact on both the FSP and affected customers
Include third-party service providers and outsourcing arrangements in scope
Develop and implement mitigation plans based on risk assessment outcomes

maturity.basic: Informal risk assessment with subjective judgements and no documented methodology
maturity.mature: Structured likelihood-impact matrix with defined scales, documented assessments, mitigation plans with owners and deadlines, covering third-party risks
maturity.advanced: Quantitative risk modelling with scenario analysis, Monte Carlo simulation for customer information breach impacts, integrated with enterprise risk management

controls.name: Proportionate Risk Assessment
controls.description: Risk assessment scaled proportionately to the FSP's operational size, nature, complexity, and the volume and sensitivity of customer information held.
keyActivities: Calibrate risk assessment depth and frequency to FSP's size and complexity
Factor in the volume and sensitivity of customer information handled
Leverage existing risk management arrangements where they address customer information risks
Document the rationale for the proportionality approach adopted

maturity.basic: One-size-fits-all risk assessment with no consideration of proportionality
maturity.mature: Risk assessment framework explicitly calibrated to FSP size, complexity, and data sensitivity, with documented proportionality rationale
maturity.advanced: Dynamic proportionality model that automatically adjusts assessment rigour based on data volume changes, business model evolution, and regulatory expectations

controls.name: Customer Information Lifecycle Policy
controls.description: Written policies and procedures covering the full lifecycle of customer information: collection, access, storage, use, transmission, sharing, disclosure, and disposal.
keyActivities: Document policies covering each stage of the customer information lifecycle
Ensure policies are proportionate to FSP size, complexity, and data sensitivity
Align policies with relevant legislation including FSA, IFSA, DFIA, PDPA
Maintain version control and change history for all policy documents

maturity.basic: Policies exist but do not cover the complete data lifecycle or are outdated
maturity.mature: Comprehensive lifecycle policies reviewed annually, proportionality documented, aligned with all applicable legislation, accessible to all staff
maturity.advanced: Policy automation with data classification-driven controls, real-time policy compliance monitoring, and integrated policy-to-control mapping

controls.name: Specific Customer Information Handling Policies
controls.description: Written policies and procedures for specific high-risk handling scenarios: off-site work arrangements, physical document transport, portable IT equipment and storage devices, and breach incident handling.
keyActivities: Establish policy for off-site work arrangements involving customer information access
Document procedures for handling and transporting physical documents containing customer information
Define rules for portable IT equipment and data storage device usage
Create and maintain a customer information breach incident handling policy

maturity.basic: Generic policies exist but do not specifically address off-site work, physical transport, portable devices, or breach handling
maturity.mature: Dedicated policies for each specified area, integrated with HR and IT policies, regularly tested through simulations and drills
maturity.advanced: Context-aware policies with automated enforcement, real-time compliance monitoring for remote work, and integrated breach response playbooks

controls.name: Policy Communication and Accessibility
controls.description: The designated officer ensures customer information policies and procedures are readily accessible and clearly communicated to all staff to drive compliance.
keyActivities: Designated officer oversees communication of all customer information policies
Policies are published on accessible platforms such as intranet or policy management system
Staff acknowledge receipt and understanding of policies
Communication effectiveness is periodically assessed

maturity.basic: Policies stored on shared drives with no active communication or acknowledgement process
maturity.mature: Centralised policy portal with version control, mandatory annual acknowledgement by all staff, and designated officer-led communication programme
maturity.advanced: AI-powered policy search, personalised policy notifications based on role, real-time compliance dashboards, and multilingual accessibility

controls.name: Continuous Policy Review
controls.description: Continuous review of written policies and procedures to ensure they remain adequate, relevant, and effective in response to changes in the operating environment.
keyActivities: Establish a formal policy review cycle triggered by regulatory changes, incidents, or operating model shifts
Assess policy adequacy, relevance, and operational effectiveness
Document review outcomes and policy amendments
Obtain appropriate approval for policy changes

maturity.basic: Policies are reviewed only when triggered by an incident or regulatory examination
maturity.mature: Annual review cycle with trigger-based interim reviews, documented assessment criteria, and formal change approval process
maturity.advanced: Continuous monitoring of regulatory changes with automated policy gap analysis, proactive policy enhancement, and benchmarking against industry practices

controls.name: Preventive and Detective ICT Controls
controls.description: Deployment of preventive ICT controls to stop theft, loss, misuse, or unauthorised access to customer information, and detective controls to identify errors and irregularities when they occur.
keyActivities: Deploy preventive ICT controls such as firewalls, encryption, and DLP
Implement detective controls including SIEM, anomaly detection, and audit logging
Regularly monitor control effectiveness against evolving threats
Update and tune controls based on threat landscape changes

maturity.basic: Basic firewall and antivirus with limited logging, no structured monitoring programme
maturity.mature: Layered preventive and detective controls with SIEM integration, regular effectiveness reviews, and documented control testing results
maturity.advanced: AI-driven threat detection, automated response orchestration, continuous control validation through breach-and-attack simulation, and real-time effectiveness metrics

controls.name: Off-site Work Arrangement Controls
controls.description: Controls for off-site work arrangements including robust authentication for remote access, encryption of data on ICT equipment, and secure data transmission for staff, representatives, and agents.
keyActivities: Implement strong multi-factor authentication for remote access to FSP systems
Encrypt data stored on all off-site ICT equipment
Ensure secure data transmission channels (VPN, TLS) for remote workers
Apply equivalent controls to representatives and agents working off-site

maturity.basic: Basic VPN access with single-factor authentication, no device encryption enforcement
maturity.mature: MFA-enforced remote access, full disk encryption on all remote devices, secure tunnelling, and endpoint compliance checks before access granted
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust architecture with continuous authentication, device posture assessment, micro-segmentation for remote access, and real-time session monitoring

controls.name: Portable Storage Device Controls
controls.description: Restriction of customer information downloads to FSP-provided portable devices on a legitimate business-need basis, with mandatory password protection and data encryption.
keyActivities: Restrict customer information downloads to FSP-approved portable devices only
Enforce business-need justification and approval for downloads
Apply mandatory encryption and password protection to portable storage devices
Consider disabling USB ports and CD writers for staff without operational need

maturity.basic: No restrictions on USB devices, portable storage not encrypted
maturity.mature: Whitelisted USB devices only, mandatory BitLocker/FileVault encryption, approval workflow for downloads, USB port restrictions for non-operational staff
maturity.advanced: Hardware-enforced device whitelisting, automated DLP scanning of all removable media, real-time alerting on unapproved device connections

controls.name: Call Recording Access Control
controls.description: Need-to-know access restriction for call recordings containing customer information from recorded telephone conversations.
keyActivities: Implement role-based access to call recording systems
Restrict access to call recordings on a strict need-to-know basis
Maintain audit logs of all call recording access
Periodically review access rights to call recording systems

maturity.basic: Call recordings accessible to broad team with no formal access restriction
maturity.mature: Role-based access with need-to-know enforcement, audit logging, and periodic access reviews
maturity.advanced: Automated access expiry, real-time monitoring of unusual access patterns, data masking in call recordings for non-authorised listeners

controls.name: Unauthorised Disclosure Deterrence Mechanisms
controls.description: Mechanisms creating a strong deterrent effect against unauthorised disclosure of customer information by staff, including awareness of disciplinary actions, CCTV, open-office layouts, and device restrictions.
keyActivities: Communicate disciplinary consequences for unauthorised disclosure to all staff
Install CCTV in relevant areas where customer information is handled
Implement open office concepts to reduce covert data exfiltration
Restrict personal electronic devices in high-risk areas such as data centres and call centres
Encourage and facilitate whistleblowing on data misuse

maturity.basic: Basic code of conduct referencing data protection, no physical deterrent measures
maturity.mature: Comprehensive deterrent programme with CCTV, personal device restrictions in high-risk zones, whistleblowing channel, and regular communication of consequences
maturity.advanced: Behavioural analytics to detect insider threat indicators, gamified reporting incentives, digital watermarking of sensitive documents, and proactive culture surveys

controls.name: Social Media and Web-based Communication Restriction
controls.description: Restriction of access to web-based communication websites and end-to-end encrypted social media platforms for staff handling customer information to prevent external data leakage.
keyActivities: Identify and categorise web-based communication and social media platforms to restrict
Block or restrict access to encrypted platforms such as WhatsApp Desktop, Facebook Messenger, and Skype Messenger
Apply restrictions specifically to staff who handle customer information
Monitor and enforce web access policies through web filtering technology

maturity.basic: No web filtering in place, social media accessible from workstations
maturity.mature: Web content filtering blocking designated social media and messaging platforms for customer-information-handling roles, with policy exception process
maturity.advanced: Context-aware web filtering with user-behaviour analytics, real-time DLP inspection of web traffic, and automated policy enforcement tied to data classification

controls.name: Unauthorised Activity Detection Mechanisms
controls.description: Detection mechanisms for unauthorised access, unusual frequent viewing, suspicious downloading activities, and unauthorised disclosure of customer information to external parties.
keyActivities: Deploy monitoring for unauthorised access to customer information
Detect unusual or frequent viewing patterns by staff
Identify suspicious downloading activities involving customer information
Monitor for unauthorised disclosure of customer information to external parties
Conduct regular audit trail reviews and random periodic sample checks

maturity.basic: Basic system logging with no active monitoring or analysis
maturity.mature: SIEM-based monitoring with defined use cases for unauthorised access, bulk downloads, and unusual viewing patterns, with regular audit trail reviews
maturity.advanced: User and entity behaviour analytics (UEBA), machine learning-driven anomaly detection, automated incident creation and response, and continuous insider threat monitoring

controls.name: Role-based Access Profiles for Customer Information
controls.description: Each job role profile includes a defined description of access rights to customer information, ensuring staff operate strictly within their authorised access boundaries.
keyActivities: Define access rights to customer information in each job role profile
Map role profiles to system access permissions
Ensure staff perform duties in accordance with their defined access rights
Review and update role profiles when job functions change

maturity.basic: Access rights assigned ad-hoc without formal role profiles
maturity.mature: Formal RBAC model with documented role profiles, access rights mapped to job descriptions, and HR-IT integration for joiners/movers/leavers
maturity.advanced: Dynamic RBAC with attribute-based access controls, automated provisioning/deprovisioning, and real-time access certification

controls.name: Multi-level System Access Controls
controls.description: Identification of customer information locations across systems with layered access controls at application, database, operating system, and network levels to prevent unauthorised access.
keyActivities: Map and inventory all locations where customer information resides
Implement access controls at application level
Implement access controls at database level
Implement access controls at operating system and network levels
Regularly test and verify multi-level access control effectiveness

maturity.basic: Access controls exist at application level only, no systematic data mapping
maturity.mature: Complete data inventory with access controls implemented and tested at all four levels (application, database, OS, network), with documented control evidence
maturity.advanced: Automated data discovery and classification, microsegmentation, zero-trust access controls at every layer, and continuous access control validation

controls.name: Periodic Access Review and Immediate Revocation
controls.description: Regular review of staff access rights to customer information with immediate revocation for departing staff or role changes that no longer require customer information access.
keyActivities: Conduct periodic access rights reviews for all staff with customer information access
Immediately revoke access for staff leaving the FSP
Immediately revoke access for staff changing to roles not requiring customer information access
Maintain audit trail of access changes and revocations

maturity.basic: Access reviews conducted annually with manual processes, revocation delayed by days
maturity.mature: Quarterly access reviews with automated joiner/mover/leaver integration, same-day revocation on departure, documented review outcomes
maturity.advanced: Continuous access monitoring with automated recertification, real-time revocation triggered by HR events, and AI-driven access anomaly detection

controls.name: Physical Security Controls for Customer Information
controls.description: Physical security controls ensuring customer information in paper and electronic forms is protected against theft, loss, misuse, or unauthorised access in all FSP premises.
keyActivities: Implement physical access controls for areas storing customer information
Protect both paper-based and electronic customer information physically
Deploy visitor management processes for areas with customer information
Regularly test and review physical security controls

maturity.basic: Basic locks on doors with no electronic access control or monitoring
maturity.mature: Electronic access control, CCTV, visitor management, secure document storage, and periodic physical security assessments
maturity.advanced: Integrated physical-logical security with biometric access, real-time occupancy monitoring, AI-powered CCTV analytics, and environmental threat detection

controls.name: Restricted Area and Intruder Deterrent Controls
controls.description: Access restriction and intruder deterrents for areas storing large volumes of or critical and sensitive customer information, such as server rooms and filing rooms.
keyActivities: Identify areas storing large volumes or sensitive customer information
Restrict access to identified areas to authorised personnel only
Deploy intruder deterrent systems such as alarms and CCTV
Maintain access logs for restricted areas

maturity.basic: Server and filing rooms locked but no electronic access logging or intruder detection
maturity.mature: Multi-factor physical access control for restricted areas, CCTV with retention, intrusion detection systems, and periodic access reviews
maturity.advanced: Biometric access, man-trap entry systems, 24/7 security monitoring, environmental sensors, and tamper-evident controls

controls.name: Clear-Desk Policy
controls.description: Implementation of clear-desk policies to minimise the risk of customer information exposure through physical documents left unattended on desks or workstations.
keyActivities: Establish and communicate a clear-desk policy for all staff handling customer information
Provide secure storage facilities for physical documents at each workstation
Conduct periodic clear-desk checks and compliance audits
Address non-compliance through awareness and escalation

maturity.basic: Clear-desk policy exists on paper but is not enforced or monitored
maturity.mature: Active clear-desk programme with regular spot checks, compliance tracking, secure storage at all workstations, and consequences for non-compliance
maturity.advanced: Automated clean-desk verification through sensors or after-hours inspections, gamified compliance programme, and integration with physical security monitoring

controls.name: Off-site Collection and Data-in-Transit Controls
controls.description: Policies, procedures, and controls for the proper handling of customer information collected off-site and in-transit, including secure storage of physical documents and protection of portable device data.
keyActivities: Establish clear procedures for off-site customer information collection
Ensure secure storage of physical documents during transit
Protect customer information stored on portable devices during transit
Monitor compliance with off-site handling procedures

maturity.basic: Informal handling of off-site data with no documented procedures
maturity.mature: Documented procedures for off-site collection and transit, encrypted portable devices, secure document carriers, and compliance monitoring
maturity.advanced: GPS-tracked secure document pouches, tamper-evident packaging, real-time device tracking, and automated compliance verification

controls.name: Data Retention Identification and Classification
controls.description: Procedures to identify customer information no longer required from operational or legal perspectives, ensuring timely transition to secure disposal.
keyActivities: Establish data retention schedules for all categories of customer information
Identify customer information no longer required operationally or legally
Flag data for secure disposal based on retention schedule expiry
Maintain records of data identified for disposal

maturity.basic: No formal retention schedule, data accumulates indefinitely
maturity.mature: Documented retention schedules aligned with legal requirements, periodic identification of data for disposal, and disposal approval workflow
maturity.advanced: Automated data lifecycle management with retention policy enforcement, data discovery tools identifying orphaned data, and real-time compliance dashboards

controls.name: Secure Data Destruction Methods
controls.description: Deployment of industry-standard data destruction methods for paper and digital records, ensuring customer information is irrecoverable, including data wiping, physical destruction, or degaussing.
keyActivities: Use industry-standard data wiping software for digital media
Apply physical destruction or degaussing for hardware disposal
Cross-cut shred paper documents containing customer information
Verify destruction is beyond recovery and irreversible
Maintain chain of custody documentation for all destruction activities

maturity.basic: Simple file deletion or reformatting used, paper documents disposed in general waste
maturity.mature: Certified data wiping tools, cross-cut shredding, degaussing for magnetic media, destruction certificates maintained, and chain of custody documented
maturity.advanced: NIST SP 800-88 compliant media sanitisation, automated destruction scheduling, blockchain-based chain of custody, and independent verification of destruction

controls.name: Outsourced Destruction Oversight
controls.description: Risk-benefit assessment of outsourced destruction providers, secure pre-collection storage in tamper-proof containers, and random checks on collection and destruction processes.
keyActivities: Conduct risk-benefit assessment before engaging outsourced destruction providers
Shred or store data in tamper-proof sealed bags or locked containers before collection
Perform random checks on outsourced collection and destruction processes
Obtain destruction certificates from outsourced service providers

maturity.basic: Outsourced destruction engaged without formal risk assessment, no verification of destruction
maturity.mature: Documented risk-benefit assessment, tamper-proof pre-collection storage, scheduled and random destruction audits, and destruction certificates filed
maturity.advanced: Witnessed destruction events, CCTV coverage of destruction process, chain-of-custody tracking from FSP premises to destruction, and provider certification requirements

controls.name: Employment Confidentiality Undertaking
controls.description: Employment contracts include a signed confidentiality undertaking specifying legal obligations to safeguard customer information and consequences for non-compliance.
keyActivities: Include confidentiality undertaking clause in all employment contracts
Specify legal obligations under FSA, IFSA, DFIA, and PDPA
Clearly state consequences for breaching confidentiality obligations
Obtain signed acknowledgement from all staff

maturity.basic: Generic confidentiality clause in employment contracts without specific legal references
maturity.mature: Detailed confidentiality undertaking referencing specific legislation, consequences documented, signed by all staff, and periodically refreshed
maturity.advanced: Role-specific confidentiality undertakings calibrated to data access levels, annual re-signing with updated obligations, and integration with access provisioning

controls.name: External Vendor Personnel Vetting and Monitoring
controls.description: Ensuring that external vendors working on FSP premises (security guards, cleaners, maintenance) vet and monitor their personnel to reduce customer information theft risk.
keyActivities: Require vendor contracts to mandate personnel vetting and monitoring
Verify vendor vetting processes cover background checks and references
Monitor vendor personnel access to areas containing customer information
Periodically review vendor compliance with vetting requirements

maturity.basic: No verification of vendor personnel vetting, unrestricted access to premises
maturity.mature: Vendor contracts require documented vetting, vendor personnel registered with building access restricted to approved areas, and periodic compliance reviews
maturity.advanced: Integration of vendor personnel into FSP identity management, real-time access monitoring, escorted-only access in sensitive areas, and continuous vendor risk assessment

controls.name: Staff Awareness Programme on Customer Information
controls.description: Maintaining a high degree of staff awareness on the need to protect customer information confidentiality and security, compliance with policies and procedures, and the consequences of breaches.
keyActivities: Communicate the importance of protecting customer information confidentiality and security
Ensure all staff understand relevant policies and procedures
Inform all staff of consequences for involvement in customer information breaches
Maintain ongoing awareness through multiple communication channels

maturity.basic: One-off email communications with no structured awareness programme
maturity.mature: Structured annual awareness programme with posters, emails, town halls, and acknowledgement tracking
maturity.advanced: Continuous engagement programme with gamification, phishing simulations focused on customer data, real-time awareness nudges, and culture measurement

controls.name: Staff Compliance Monitoring Mechanisms
controls.description: Robust monitoring mechanisms to ensure staff adhere to established policies, procedures, and controls for customer information protection.
keyActivities: Implement monitoring mechanisms for staff compliance with data protection policies
Conduct periodic compliance checks and spot audits
Analyse monitoring results and identify non-compliance trends
Escalate systemic non-compliance to senior management

maturity.basic: No formal compliance monitoring, issues detected only through incidents
maturity.mature: Structured compliance monitoring programme with scheduled spot checks, automated policy compliance reporting, and trend analysis
maturity.advanced: Continuous compliance monitoring with AI-driven behavioural analysis, real-time policy violation alerts, and predictive non-compliance indicators

controls.name: Customer Information Protection Training Programme
controls.description: Relevant training and regular reminders to all staff on their obligations to safeguard customer information confidentiality, including specific onboarding training for new staff.
keyActivities: Deliver regular training to all staff on customer information protection obligations
Include specific data protection training in new staff onboarding programmes
Notify new staff of possible actions for non-compliance with policies
Ensure guidance materials are concise and reader-friendly

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc training with no structured curriculum or onboarding component
maturity.mature: Annual training programme with mandatory completion tracking, dedicated onboarding module, reader-friendly guides, and regular refresher reminders
maturity.advanced: Role-specific training paths, scenario-based learning with case studies, microlearning modules, and adaptive training based on individual risk profiles

controls.name: Training Effectiveness Measurement
controls.description: Mechanisms to gauge the effectiveness of customer information protection training, including annual awareness surveys to assess staff understanding.
keyActivities: Conduct annual awareness surveys to assess staff understanding
Measure training completion rates and assessment scores
Analyse survey results to identify knowledge gaps
Use effectiveness data to improve training content and delivery

maturity.basic: Training delivered with no effectiveness measurement
maturity.mature: Annual awareness surveys, post-training assessments, completion tracking, and gap analysis used to improve training programme
maturity.advanced: Continuous effectiveness measurement with phishing simulation results, behavioural analytics, comparative benchmarking, and predictive modelling of awareness levels

controls.name: Staff Breach Investigation and Disciplinary Action
controls.description: Thorough and timely investigation of customer information breaches by staff with appropriate disciplinary actions that serve as a strong deterrent, including Board reporting of outcomes.
keyActivities: Conduct thorough and timely investigation upon detecting staff-related breaches
Take appropriate disciplinary actions against staff involved in breaches
Ensure actions serve as a deterrent to all staff
Document rationale and obtain senior management approval if no action is taken
Report investigation results and actions to the Board

maturity.basic: Ad-hoc investigation with inconsistent disciplinary responses
maturity.mature: Formal investigation framework with standard process, consistent disciplinary matrix, documented decisions, senior management sign-off, and Board reporting
maturity.advanced: Forensic investigation capability, independent investigation team, integration with HR and legal, lessons-learnt dissemination, and trend analysis across investigations

controls.name: Representative and Agent Accountability
controls.description: FSP retains full accountability for the conduct of its appointed representatives and agents regarding any customer information breach, with appropriate contractual and monitoring controls.
keyActivities: Define accountability framework for representatives and agents in contracts
Extend customer information protection policies to representatives and agents
Monitor representative and agent compliance with customer information obligations
Investigate and remediate any breaches by representatives or agents

maturity.basic: Generic contractual obligations with no specific customer information accountability provisions
maturity.mature: Detailed contractual obligations, representative and agent training, periodic compliance monitoring, and incident response covering representatives and agents
maturity.advanced: Real-time monitoring of representative and agent data access, behavioural analytics, and integrated accountability framework across all distribution channels

controls.name: Biennial Independent Review of Controls
controls.description: Independent review of customer information protection policies, procedures, and controls at least once every two years, conducted by a function independent of the business units handling customer information.
keyActivities: Schedule and conduct independent review at least biennially
Engage an independent function such as internal audit to perform the review
Assess the scope, methodology, and evidence base for the review
Document and report findings to senior management and the Board

maturity.basic: No formal independent review or reviews conducted only when triggered by regulator
maturity.mature: Biennial independent review by internal audit with structured scope, documented methodology, findings, and Board reporting
maturity.advanced: Annual independent review with combined internal audit and external specialist input, risk-based scope selection, and continuous assurance elements

controls.name: Senior Management and Control Effectiveness Assessment
controls.description: The independent review assesses senior management effectiveness, their oversight, and the adequacy and effectiveness of measures protecting customer information.
keyActivities: Assess senior management effectiveness in overseeing customer information protection
Evaluate the adequacy and effectiveness of all protective measures
Benchmark controls against regulatory requirements and industry practices
Provide recommendations for improving oversight and control effectiveness

maturity.basic: Review focuses on control existence only, does not assess management effectiveness
maturity.mature: Comprehensive review covering both control adequacy and management oversight effectiveness, with actionable recommendations
maturity.advanced: Multi-dimensional effectiveness assessment with quantified metrics, maturity scoring, peer benchmarking, and forward-looking risk assessment

controls.name: Independent Review Findings Communication and Remediation
controls.description: Communication of written independent review findings to senior management and the Board, with timely corrective actions to address identified deficiencies.
keyActivities: Prepare written findings including scope, methodology, evidence, and limitations
Communicate findings to both senior management and the Board
Develop and implement corrective action plans for identified deficiencies
Track remediation progress and report on completion

maturity.basic: Findings shared informally with no structured remediation tracking
maturity.mature: Formal written report presented to senior management and Board, corrective action plans with owners and deadlines, and progress tracking through to closure
maturity.advanced: Automated remediation workflow, real-time tracking dashboard, root cause analysis integration, and systemic issue identification across multiple review cycles

controls.name: Customer Information Breach Response Plan
controls.description: Comprehensive breach handling and response plan covering both deliberate and non-deliberate customer information breaches, with escalation procedures and clear lines of responsibility.
keyActivities: Develop and maintain a customer information breach response plan
Include procedures for both deliberate and non-deliberate breaches
Define escalation procedures and clear responsibility lines
Establish containment and remediation protocols to prevent recurrence
Test the response plan through periodic drills and simulations

maturity.basic: No formal breach response plan, ad-hoc response to incidents
maturity.mature: Documented breach response plan with escalation matrix, role assignments, containment procedures, and annual tabletop exercises
maturity.advanced: Automated incident response workflows, integrated with SIEM and SOAR, regular red-team exercises, and continuous plan improvement based on lessons learnt

controls.name: Breach Escalation Awareness and Staff Training
controls.description: All staff understand escalation procedures and relevant staff are trained to effectively take appropriate remedial actions to protect affected customers during a breach.
keyActivities: Communicate breach escalation procedures to all staff
Provide specialised training to incident response team members
Conduct breach response drills to test escalation effectiveness
Ensure trained staff can protect affected customers' interests during a breach

maturity.basic: Staff unaware of escalation procedures, no breach-specific training
maturity.mature: Annual breach escalation training for all staff, specialised incident response training for key personnel, and periodic simulation exercises
maturity.advanced: Continuous breach preparedness programme with role-specific scenarios, cross-functional exercises, and real-time escalation testing

controls.name: Breach Detection and Identification Mechanism
controls.description: Mechanisms to identify customer information breaches including those arising from customer complaints, with prompt and proper investigation of complaints.
keyActivities: Implement automated breach detection mechanisms
Establish channels for breach reporting by staff and customers
Integrate customer complaint analysis with breach detection
Promptly and properly investigate all complaints that may indicate a breach

maturity.basic: Breaches detected only through customer complaints with no proactive monitoring
maturity.mature: Multi-channel detection including automated monitoring, staff reporting hotline, customer complaint triage, and systematic investigation process
maturity.advanced: AI-powered anomaly detection, integrated threat intelligence, predictive breach indicators, and automated triage and classification

controls.name: Immediate Breach Containment
controls.description: Immediate mitigating actions to contain a customer information breach, including cross-FSP notification where another FSP's customer data is involved.
keyActivities: Take immediate action to contain and limit the scope of a breach
Preserve evidence for subsequent investigation
Notify affected FSPs promptly where cross-FSP customer data is involved
Implement temporary controls to prevent further exposure while permanent remediation is developed

maturity.basic: Slow ad-hoc containment with no pre-defined procedures
maturity.mature: Pre-defined containment playbooks for common breach scenarios, cross-FSP notification procedures, and evidence preservation protocols
maturity.advanced: Automated containment actions triggered by detection systems, real-time isolation capabilities, and coordinated cross-FSP incident response

controls.name: Breach Impact Assessment
controls.description: Structured assessment of breach impact considering whether the breach was accidental or intentional, data sensitivity, number of affected customers, exposure recipients, and fraud risk.
keyActivities: Determine whether the breach was accidental or intentional
Assess the type and sensitivity of customer information involved
Quantify the number of affected customers
Evaluate who the information was exposed to and the fraud risk
Obtain designated senior officer confirmation of significant harm assessment

maturity.basic: Informal impact assessment with no structured criteria
maturity.mature: Structured impact assessment template aligned with MCIPD criteria, senior officer sign-off, and documented assessment rationale
maturity.advanced: Automated impact scoring with data classification integration, real-time affected customer quantification, and predictive fraud risk modelling

controls.name: BNM Breach Notification
controls.description: Immediate notification to BNM for breaches posing reputational risk, causing significant harm, or affecting over 1,000 customers, with prescribed minimum content and phased disclosure provisions.
keyActivities: Assess breach against BNM notification triggers (reputational risk, significant harm, significant scale)
Prepare notification with prescribed minimum content
Submit immediate notification to BNM even if complete information is not yet available
Provide estimated affected customer numbers with commitment to finalise
Submit complete information to BNM as soon as obtained
Notify law enforcement if fraud, criminal activity, or identity theft is suspected

maturity.basic: No defined process for BNM notification, reactive and delayed submissions
maturity.mature: Pre-drafted notification templates, clear trigger assessment criteria, designated officer approval workflow, and phased notification procedures
maturity.advanced: Automated trigger assessment, pre-populated notification templates with real-time data feeds, integrated regulatory submission portal, and dual-track BNM and law enforcement notification

controls.name: Breach Investigation and Board Reporting
controls.description: Root cause investigation by competent party with independent oversight, completion within three months, detailed investigation report tabled to the Board, and submission to BNM within one working day.
keyActivities: Appoint a competent party to investigate with independent oversight
Ascertain root causes and determine remedial actions to prevent recurrence
Complete investigation within three months of breach detection
Table detailed investigation report to the Board
Submit report and Appendix 1 to BNM within one working day of Board tabling
Obtain designated senior officer sign-off on the report

maturity.basic: Informal investigation with no independent oversight or structured reporting
maturity.mature: Formal investigation framework with competent investigator, independent oversight, root cause analysis, three-month timeline tracking, and Board and BNM reporting workflow
maturity.advanced: Digital forensic investigation capability, automated evidence collection, integrated case management, and continuous improvement loop from investigation outcomes

controls.name: Breach Register and Records Retention
controls.description: Maintenance of a register recording all customer information breaches with root causes, remedial actions, and lessons learnt, and retention of investigation reports for at least seven years.
keyActivities: Maintain a comprehensive breach register for all customer information incidents
Record root causes, remedial actions, and lessons learnt for each breach
Retain investigation reports for at least seven years from Board tabling date
Analyse breach register data for trends and systemic issues

maturity.basic: Informal incident log with incomplete records and no trend analysis
maturity.mature: Structured breach register with mandatory fields for root cause, remedial actions, and lessons learnt, seven-year retention enforced, and periodic trend analysis
maturity.advanced: Digital breach register with automated data capture, real-time trend analytics, predictive risk indicators, and integration with risk management system

controls.name: Customer Breach Notification
controls.description: Direct notification to affected customers without undue delay for breaches causing significant harm, with prescribed minimum content in plain language and provisions for public announcement when direct notification is impractical.
keyActivities: Notify affected customers directly without undue delay after BNM notification
Include prescribed content: breach description, consequences, protective steps, remedial measures, and contact details
Ensure notification is clear and in plain language
Issue public announcement if direct notification requires disproportionate effort or affected customers cannot yet be identified
Train staff to handle customer queries and provide assistance on protective measures

maturity.basic: No defined customer notification process, delayed and inconsistent communication
maturity.mature: Pre-drafted notification templates in plain language, multi-channel notification capability, public announcement procedures, and trained helpdesk staff
maturity.advanced: Automated personalised customer notifications, multi-language support, dedicated breach response call centre, proactive customer protection measures, and real-time notification tracking

controls.name: OSP Risk Monitoring
controls.description: Continuous monitoring of risks arising from outsourced service providers handling customer information, with proactive identification and management of emerging risks.
keyActivities: Identify and categorise risks from each OSP handling customer information
Implement ongoing monitoring of OSP risk indicators
Escalate emerging OSP risks to senior management
Adjust monitoring intensity based on risk assessment outcomes

maturity.basic: No structured OSP risk monitoring, reactive only
maturity.mature: Structured risk monitoring programme with defined KRIs for each OSP, periodic risk reporting, and escalation procedures
maturity.advanced: Real-time OSP risk monitoring through integrated dashboards, automated risk scoring, threat intelligence feeds on OSP vulnerabilities, and continuous assurance

controls.name: OSP Due Diligence Assessment
controls.description: Adequate and relevant due diligence when selecting OSPs with access to customer information, assessing risks introduced and determining appropriate ongoing monitoring.
keyActivities: Conduct comprehensive due diligence before appointing OSPs with customer data access
Assess the level of risk each OSP may introduce
Evaluate OSP capability in processing, storing, and disposing of customer information
Determine appropriate ongoing monitoring based on due diligence outcomes

maturity.basic: Minimal due diligence limited to financial checks only
maturity.mature: Structured due diligence framework covering security posture, compliance, financial stability, and operational capability, with risk-based monitoring determination
maturity.advanced: Continuous due diligence with real-time security posture assessment, automated risk scoring, and dynamic monitoring calibration

controls.name: OSP Comparable Controls Verification
controls.description: Verification that OSPs maintain policies, procedures, and controls comparable to the FSP's own standards for safeguarding customer information at all times.
keyActivities: Define minimum control standards for OSPs based on FSP's own standards
Verify OSP policies, procedures, and controls meet comparable standards
Communicate clear expectations on control measures for processing, storage, transmission, disposal, and destruction
Periodically reassess OSP control adequacy

maturity.basic: No assessment of OSP control standards, assumed adequate
maturity.mature: Documented minimum control standards, initial and periodic control assessments, and gap remediation tracking
maturity.advanced: Continuous OSP control monitoring, automated compliance verification, SOC 2 report analysis, and control effectiveness benchmarking

controls.name: OSP Service Level Agreement Requirements
controls.description: Comprehensive SLA provisions ensuring OSP obligations for customer information safeguarding, personnel vetting, access restrictions, breach reporting, data return/destruction, and FSP audit rights.
keyActivities: Include mandatory customer information safeguarding provisions in all OSP SLAs
Require personnel vetting and access restrictions in SLA
Mandate breach investigation and reporting within agreed timeframes
Include data return or destruction upon SLA termination
Ensure FSP audit and inspection rights are contractually guaranteed
Amend existing SLAs to comply, not only upon renewal

maturity.basic: Generic SLA with no specific customer information safeguarding provisions
maturity.mature: Comprehensive SLA template covering all MCIPD paragraph 12.4 requirements, existing SLAs amended, and compliance tracked
maturity.advanced: Dynamic SLA management with automated compliance monitoring, real-time breach reporting integration, and contractual performance analytics

controls.name: OSP Non-Disclosure Agreement
controls.description: Binding non-disclosure undertaking executed by all OSPs handling customer information.
keyActivities: Execute binding NDA with every OSP handling customer information
Ensure NDA covers the full scope of customer information handling
Include post-termination non-disclosure obligations
Maintain NDA register and track renewal/expiry dates

maturity.basic: Generic NDA not specific to customer information handling
maturity.mature: Customer-information-specific NDA template, executed with all relevant OSPs, with register tracking and renewal management
maturity.advanced: Automated NDA lifecycle management, compliance monitoring, and integration with vendor risk management system

controls.name: OSP Personnel Training and Awareness
controls.description: Ensuring OSPs provide regular training to their personnel on customer information handling policies and procedures, with periodic review of training programme effectiveness.
keyActivities: Require OSPs to deliver regular customer information protection training
Review adequacy and effectiveness of OSP training programmes
Consider providing FSP-delivered training to OSP personnel
Track OSP training completion and effectiveness metrics

maturity.basic: No visibility into OSP training programmes
maturity.mature: Contractual training requirements, periodic review of OSP training materials and completion records, and FSP-provided supplementary training
maturity.advanced: Joint FSP-OSP training programme, FSP-administered assessments for OSP personnel, and continuous training effectiveness monitoring

controls.name: OSP Biennial Compliance Review
controls.description: Review of each OSP at least once every two years to confirm fulfilment of contractual obligations in safeguarding customer information.
keyActivities: Schedule and conduct OSP reviews at least biennially
Assess OSP compliance with SLA customer information provisions
Document review findings and required remediation actions
Track remediation to closure and report to senior management

maturity.basic: No regular OSP reviews conducted
maturity.mature: Biennial review programme with structured assessment criteria, documented findings, remediation tracking, and senior management reporting
maturity.advanced: Annual reviews supplemented by continuous monitoring, risk-based review scope, third-party audit reports (SOC 2/ISO 27001), and integrated vendor risk dashboard

controls.name: OSP Data Sharing Records and Audit Trail
controls.description: Maintenance of accurate and complete records and audit trails of all customer information shared with or provided to OSPs.
keyActivities: Maintain records of all customer information shared with each OSP
Ensure completeness and accuracy of data sharing records
Implement audit trail for data transfers to OSPs
Periodically reconcile data sharing records with OSP holdings

maturity.basic: No systematic tracking of customer information shared with OSPs
maturity.mature: Data sharing register maintained for each OSP, audit trail for data transfers, periodic reconciliation, and documented data inventory
maturity.advanced: Automated data lineage tracking, real-time data flow monitoring, data loss prevention at transfer points, and blockchain-based audit trail

controls.name: Customer Consent Management
controls.description: Processes for obtaining, recording, and managing customer consent for disclosure of their information, ensuring informed and valid consent in compliance with applicable law.
keyActivities: Obtain informed customer consent before disclosing information where required
Record and maintain evidence of consent with timestamps and scope
Implement consent withdrawal mechanisms for customers
Periodically review consent records for validity and currency

maturity.basic: Consent captured informally with no systematic record-keeping
maturity.mature: Digital consent management platform with audit trail, consent version control, withdrawal mechanism, and periodic validity review
maturity.advanced: Granular consent management with preference centre, real-time consent enforcement at disclosure points, and automated consent lifecycle management

controls.name: Cross-Border Disclosure Controls
controls.description: Controls governing the disclosure of customer information across jurisdictional boundaries, including regulatory approval requirements, data protection adequacy assessments, and additional safeguards.
keyActivities: Identify and classify all cross-border customer information disclosures
Assess data protection adequacy of receiving jurisdiction
Obtain regulatory approval where required
Implement additional safeguards such as contractual provisions and encryption for cross-border transfers

maturity.basic: No process for managing cross-border disclosures, handled ad-hoc
maturity.mature: Documented cross-border disclosure policy, jurisdiction adequacy register, regulatory approval workflow, and additional safeguard implementation
maturity.advanced: Automated data transfer impact assessments, real-time cross-border flow monitoring, dynamic safeguard application based on jurisdiction risk rating

controls.name: Permitted Disclosure Record-Keeping
controls.description: Comprehensive record-keeping of all permitted disclosures of customer information, including legal basis, recipient details, data disclosed, and approval evidence.
keyActivities: Record all permitted disclosures with legal basis, recipient, data scope, and date
Maintain approval evidence for each disclosure
Implement a centralised disclosure register accessible for audit
Periodically review disclosure records for completeness and compliance

maturity.basic: Disclosures not systematically recorded or tracked
maturity.mature: Centralised disclosure register with mandatory fields for legal basis, recipient, scope, approval evidence, and periodic compliance review
maturity.advanced: Automated disclosure logging integrated with data transfer systems, real-time compliance monitoring, and analytics on disclosure patterns
